title: Fed Minutes: No Q1 Rate Cuts — S&P 500 Falls 1.2% | Finance Daily Briefing Jan 15 2025
description:
Federal Reserve December FOMC minutes released today confirmed officials see no urgency to cut interest rates in early 2025. The S&P 500 fell 1.2% in response, the US dollar index climbed to a two-month high, and bond yields rose across the curve as markets repriced rate expectations sharply.

In today's briefing:
- FOMC minutes rule out a Q1 rate cut — Fed stays higher for longer
- S&P 500 drops 1.2% with rate-sensitive sectors leading losses
- US dollar index hits two-month high; EUR/USD dips below 1.09
- Treasury yields rise across the curve on hawkish Fed signal
- Tomorrow's watchpoints: Fed speaker commentary and any policy tone shifts

Stay informed — subscribe for the daily finance briefing and never miss a market-moving macro event.
